paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: These are the words of the covenant which Jehovah commanded Moses to make with the children of Israel in the land of Moab, besides the covenant which he made with them in Horeb.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses called unto all Israel, and said unto them, Ye have seen all that Jehovah did before your eyes in the land of Egypt unto Pharaoh, and unto all his servants, and unto all his land;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: the great trials which thine eyes saw, the signs, and those great wonders:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: but Jehovah hath not given you a heart to know, and eyes to see, and ears to hear, unto this day.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And I have led you forty years in the wilderness: your clothes are not waxed old upon you, and thy shoe is not waxed old upon thy foot.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ye have not eaten bread, neither have ye drunk wine or strong drink; that ye may know that I am Jehovah your God.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And when ye came unto this place, Sihon the king of Heshbon, and Og the king of Bashan, came out against us unto battle, and we smote them:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and we took their land, and gave it for an inheritance unto the Reubenites, and to the Gadites, and to the half-tribe of the Manassites.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Keep therefore the words of this covenant, and do them, that ye may prosper in all that ye do.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ye stand this day all of you before Jehovah your God; your heads, your tribes, your elders, and your officers, even all the men of Israel,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: your little ones, your wives, and thy sojourner that is in the midst of thy camps, from the hewer of thy wood unto the drawer of thy water;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: that thou mayest enter into the covenant of Jehovah thy God, and into his oath, which Jehovah thy God maketh with thee this day;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: that he may establish thee this day unto himself for a people, and that he may be unto thee a God, as he spake unto thee, and as he sware unto thy fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Neither with you only do I make this covenant and this oath,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: but with him that standeth here with us this day before Jehovah our God, and also with him that is not here with us this day

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: (for ye know how we dwelt in the land of Egypt, and how we came through the midst of the nations through which ye passed;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and ye have seen their abominations, and their idols, wood and stone, silver and gold, which were among them);

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: lest there should be among you man, or woman, or family, or tribe, whose heart turneth away this day from Jehovah our God, to go to serve the gods of those nations; lest there should be among you a root that beareth gall and wormwood;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and it come to pass, when he heareth the words of this curse, that he bless himself in his heart, saying, I shall have peace, though I walk in the stubbornness of my heart, to destroy the moist with the dry.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Jehovah will not pardon him, but then the anger of Jehovah and his jealousy will smoke against that man, and all the curse that is written in this book shall lie upon him, and Jehovah will blot out his name from under heaven.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah will set him apart unto evil out of all the tribes of Israel, according to all the curses of the covenant that is written in this book of the law.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the generation to come, your children that shall rise up after you, and the foreigner that shall come from a far land, shall say, when they see the plagues of that land, and the sicknesses wherewith Jehovah hath made it sick;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: [and that] the whole land thereof is brimstone, and salt, [and] a burning, [that] it is not sown, nor beareth, nor any grass groweth therein, like the overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboiim, which Jehovah overthrew in his anger, and in his wrath:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: even all the nations shall say, Wherefore hath Jehovah done thus unto this land? what meaneth the heat of this great anger?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Then men shall say, Because they forsook the covenant of Jehovah, the God of their fathers, which he made with them when he brought them forth out of the land of Egypt,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and went and served other gods, and worshipped them, gods that they knew not, and that he had not given unto them:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: therefore the anger of Jehovah was kindled against this land, to bring upon it all the curse that is written in this book;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and Jehovah rooted them out of their land in anger, and in wrath, and in great indignation, and cast them into another land, as at this day.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The secret things belong unto Jehovah our God; but the things that are revealed belong unto us and to our children for ever, that we may do all the words of this law.
